title: gluta coarctata
intro: Gluta coarctata, also known as the Tengkawang tree or Asian stinkwood, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. Traditionally, the plant's heartwood has been used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Gluta coarctata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The plant is often prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support cardiovascular health and treat various health conditions.
